candidate_problems.text: Can the conjecture be made mathematically noncircular by defining a privileged self-modeling partition, 'predictive/control power,' and 'provable degradation under all alternative decompositions' in a way that is representation-invariant and not dependent on the analyst’s chosen state description? The core tension is whether irreducible perspectivality is an objective system property or an artifact of how we coarse-grain, encode variables, and specify tasks.

Is indexical self-modeling actually necessary for the relevant functional regime, or can every purportedly conscious-looking global model be re-expressed as distributed coordination with relational variables and no privileged 'this-system' coordinate? The open question is whether there exist concrete counterexamples either way: systems with strong self-maintenance and unified control but no irreducible indexical state, or systems with irreducible indexical structure that are intuitively nonconscious.

What empirical or computational decomposition test could discriminate conscious from nonconscious systems without collapsing into a restatement of integration, recurrence, or task complexity? The unresolved tension is operational: how to design interventions, benchmarks, or impossibility results that show irrecoverable loss under repartition specifically because of a first-person coordinate, rather than because decomposition simply damages any tightly coupled controller.
